metadata.title: Make AI Safe by Taking It Out of the Loop

beats.narration_text: Selam, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The AI needs 38 milliseconds to answer and the machine must stop in 5 — and the fix is not a faster model.

props.command: My safety system needs to respond in 5 ms but my AI model takes 38 ms. How do I make this safe?
output: Remove the AI from the hard-deadline loop. Run it async as an advisor. A deterministic detector inside the 1 kHz loop holds the guarantee.

beats.narration_text: From the book: three parallel timelines on a CNC mill. The one-kilohertz control loop never touches the AI. The AI runs async, updates a shared probability register, and stays off the critical path.

Three timelines. Top: the control loop ticking every millisecond — 1 kilohertz, 1000 decisions per second. Middle: rule-based detector, sub-millisecond trip, always inside the 5-millisecond deadline. Bottom: the AI inference bar — 38 milliseconds. That bar doesn't fit. It blows past the deadline by 7.6 times. Not a rounding error. A structural impossibility.

The fix: decouple them. The AI runs asynchronously on its own thread. Every 38 milliseconds — or whenever it finishes — it writes a fault probability field. Zero-point-three. Zero-point-seven. Whatever the model outputs. The control loop, ticking every millisecond, reads that field without blocking. It doesn't wait for the AI. It reads the last value the AI wrote. The AI is now an advisor, not a gatekeeper.

The deterministic detector trips if any sensor reading crosses a hard threshold — always inside 1 millisecond. The AI improves detection rate: it catches precursor patterns the rule can't see. But the safety guarantee never depends on the AI responding on time. The control loop doesn't care if the AI is slow, frozen, or producing garbage — the trip wire still works.

Your turn. Ask Claude: My control loop runs at open bracket freq close bracket Hz with a open bracket deadline ms close bracket millisecond deadline. My AI model takes open bracket inference ms close bracket ms. Walk me through: one — async vs sync inference architectures. Two — how the AI advisory pattern works: write-probability field, deterministic trip wire. Three — what the safety argument looks like for each. Four — what happens if the AI advisory freezes or produces garbage.

props.command: My control loop runs at [freq] Hz with a [deadline_ms] ms deadline. My AI model takes [inference_ms] ms. Walk me through: (1) async vs sync inference architectures, (2) how the AI advisory pattern works (write-probability field, deterministic trip wire), (3) what the safety argument looks like for each, (4) what happens if the AI advisory freezes or produces garbage.
output: AI inference: 38 ms. Rule-based: <1 ms. Control loop: 1 ms tick. Only the rule-based path meets the 5 ms deadline — AI must be advisory.

props.title: Make AI Safe by Taking It Out of the Loop
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
